title: anacamptis papilionacea var. expansa
intro: Anacamptis papilionacea var. expansa, also known as the butterfly orchid, is a rare and endangered subspecies of flowering plant native to the Mediterranean region. This orchid is prized for its unique, long-stemmed flowers with a distinctive shape, resembling a butterfly in flight. The butterfly orchid has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. It can be prepared into herbal remedies such as teas, infusions, and tinctures to alleviate various health issues, including fever, respiratory problems, and digestive issues, but more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
